inspirationReferences.description: Mid-century American commercial architecture characterized by upswept roofs, boomerang shapes, parabolic curves, and space-age optimism. Exemplified by the LAX Theme Building, diners like Norms, and Googie's coffee shop in Los Angeles.
elementsAdapted: Swooping parabolic top navigation bar with curved underside (header-swoosh element mimics upswept rooflines)
Boomerang-shaped panel containers with asymmetric border-radius (0 40px 0 40px)
Angled tab shapes using CSS clip-path polygon to emulate canted signage and fins
Turquoise and coral color palette drawn from period-typical candy-colored building facades

inspirationReferences.description: The 1962 Hanna-Barbera animated series depicting an optimistic space-age future. Its visual language of orbital paths, transparent tubes, capsule vehicles, and atomic motifs defined pop-culture retro-futurism.
elementsAdapted: Orbital stat rings with animated electron dots circling central metrics (inspired by atomic orbit diagrams)
Space pods as project containers — capsule-shaped cards with porthole icons
Orbital lanes for kanban columns — representing launch-to-landing trajectories
Mission control / cockpit metaphor for project workspace with instrument gauges and readouts
Space radio communication interface for AI chat with signal wave animations

inspirationReferences.description: The post-WWII atomic age spawned a distinct graphic style featuring starburst shapes, atomic orbit diagrams, boomerang patterns, and constellation/star motifs in everything from advertising to wallpaper.
elementsAdapted: Starburst dividers using CSS clip-path star polygons with pulsing animation
Atomic orbit decorative rings around avatars and stat displays
Star map / constellation whiteboard with twinkling star nodes and dashed connection lines
Satellite dish decorative elements atop schema planner entity blocks
Electron-pulse animations on chart bar tips and frequency indicators
CSS radial-gradient starfield background (no images) evoking space-age wonder

uniqueDesignElements.description: Fixed top navigation bar with a CSS-generated parabolic underside curve (border-radius 50% bottom) that creates a Googie architectural silhouette. Tabs use clip-path polygon for angled shapes rather than standard rectangles.
uniqueDesignElements.differentiator: Unlike conventional horizontal navbars or Pass 1's bottom jukebox bar, this creates an architectural profile reminiscent of mid-century coffee shop rooflines.

uniqueDesignElements.description: Dashboard statistics displayed as SVG circular rings with animated stroke-dashoffset fills. Small electron dots (SVG circles) orbit the rings using CSS transform-origin rotation, creating a live atomic-model effect.
uniqueDesignElements.differentiator: Combines data visualization with decorative atomic-age theming. The electrons are functional (they indicate activity) while also being purely ornamental, blurring the line between UI and art.

uniqueDesignElements.description: The whiteboard view presents a deep-space-navy canvas with draggable star nodes connected by SVG dashed lines with flowing stroke-dashoffset animation. Stars twinkle independently with staggered CSS animations.
uniqueDesignElements.differentiator: Transforms a standard whiteboard into an interactive star chart. The flowing dashed connections create a sense of data traveling between nodes like signals between stars.

uniqueDesignElements.description: All major panels use an asymmetric border-radius of (0 40px 0 40px), creating a distinctive boomerang / swept profile that differs from standard rounded rectangles.
uniqueDesignElements.differentiator: This single shape property creates visual consistency across all views while immediately evoking mid-century modern furniture and architecture shapes. No other pass uses this asymmetric radius.

uniqueDesignElements.description: AI chat styled as radio communications with a signal-wave equalizer header (CSS scaleY animation), frequency display, and wave-bar indicators on each message. Outgoing messages use coral tones, incoming use turquoise.
uniqueDesignElements.differentiator: Reimagines chat UI as a radio communications console rather than a standard messenger. The directional color coding (coral for transmitted, turquoise for received) reinforces the radio metaphor.

uniqueDesignElements.description: Settings toggles feature a planetary orbit metaphor — a small planet (circle) slides along an orbital track (pill shape) with smooth easing. Active state changes the track to turquoise.
uniqueDesignElements.differentiator: Standard toggle switches are reimagined as celestial mechanics. Combined with rotatable dial knobs and gauge needles, the settings view becomes a spacecraft control console.
